notes: Silver DQ rules for base.onet_experience_profiles. Thresholds recalibrated against real Bronze ingest: 878 O*NET detail codes truncate to 765 BLS SOCs (not the spec's original ~867). Row count range 720-810 accommodates version variance. Spot checks assert tier values (not category values) because real-data weighted medians differ from intuition (e.g., Retail Salespersons 41-2031 has bimodal RW distribution with weighted median category = 5, not 1-3; still 'entry' tier). Tier thresholds (0-1/1-4/4-8/8+) and 'Over 10 years' midpoint = 12 are human-approved in governance/approvals/onet-experience-requirements-open-decisions.md.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: row count 720-810
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 720 OR cnt > 810 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_experience_profiles)
rules.description: Silver row count must be in [720, 810]. Grain is one row per BLS SOC. The 878 O*NET-SOC details in Bronze collapse to 765 BLS 6-digit roots via XX-XXXX.XX -> XX-XXXX truncation.
rules.rationale: EDA: '878 distinct O*NET-SOC codes across all scales; 765 distinct BLS-SOC codes (after 7-char truncation). This will be the Silver row count, not 867.' EDA recommendation: 'rewrite from 800 <= count <= 900 to 720 <= count <= 810 (observed 765 BLS-SOC roots).' Range gives ~45-row headroom on each side. Threshold: count in [720, 810].
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §2. Occupation Coverage + §Implications for DQ Rules

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: bls_soc_code format XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(bls_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every bls_soc_code must match BLS SOC 2018 format XX-XXXX (2-digit major group, hyphen, 4-digit detail). This is the join key to Gold consumable.career_branches and to other BLS-keyed Silver tables.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Truncation to first 7 characters produces XX-XXXX. Zero format violations expected.' Source O*NET-SOC codes all follow XX-XXXX.XX, so the BLS truncation is deterministic and cannot produce malformed codes unless the transformer is buggy. Sibling rule SLV-ONET-001 enforces the same constraint on base.onet_occupations. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: bls_soc_code uniqueness (grain integrity)
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.onet_experience_profiles GROUP BY bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per bls_soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a multi-detail aggregation or promote failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: '765 distinct BLS SOC codes (after 7-char truncation)' and '63 BLS SOCs (8.2%) have multiple O*NET detail codes' -- the Silver transformer collapses those details via unweighted average, so the final row count must be 1:1 with bls_soc_code. Spec §Zone 2 declares dedup grain [bls_soc_code]. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: experience_years_typical range 0-15
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE experience_years_typical IS NULL OR experience_years_typical < 0.0 OR experience_years_typical > 15.0
rules.description: experience_years_typical must be in [0, 15]. Values come from the approved category-to-midpoint mapping where the maximum midpoint is 12 years (category 11 = 'Over 10 years'). Multi-detail unweighted averaging preserves the range since all inputs are in [0, 12].
rules.rationale: Approved midpoint table (governance/approvals/onet-experience-requirements-open-decisions.md): min=0 (categories 1-2), max=12 (category 11, 'Over 10 years'). Unweighted averaging across details cannot produce values outside [0, 12]. 15 is chosen as the validity ceiling to allow for future midpoint changes without rewriting this rule. Threshold: 0 violations.
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §6. Known-Value Spot Checks (max observed midpoint = 12)

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: experience_tier in ('entry','early','mid','senior')
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE experience_tier IS NULL OR experience_tier NOT IN ('entry', 'early', 'mid', 'senior')
rules.description: experience_tier must be exactly one of the four human-approved values: 'entry' (0-1 yr), 'early' (1-4 yr), 'mid' (4-8 yr), 'senior' (8+ yr). Tier is derived deterministically from experience_years_typical by the Silver transformer.
rules.rationale: Tier thresholds approved 2026-04-16 in governance/approvals/onet-experience-requirements-open-decisions.md. Spec §Zone 2 hardcodes these four values in transformer logic and MCP response example. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: experience_category_median range 1-11
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE experience_category_median IS NULL OR experience_category_median < 1 OR experience_category_median > 11
rules.description: experience_category_median must be in [1, 11] (valid RW category range). Values outside this range indicate a weighted-median computation error.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'RW has exactly 11 categories per occupation, min/max [1, 11] with no gaps.' The weighted-median walk produces one of the 11 valid categories; any result outside [1, 11] is a transformer bug. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: all 4 tiers represented
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT experience_tier) = 4 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE experience_tier IN ('entry', 'early', 'mid', 'senior')
rules.description: All four experience tiers (entry, early, mid, senior) must be represented in Silver. A missing tier suggests a threshold mapping bug or unusual source data where the full occupational spectrum is not covered.
rules.rationale: EDA spot checks confirm all four tiers will be present in real data: Chief Executives (senior), Software Developers (mid), Retail Salespersons (entry). Implicit 'early' tier is well-populated given 1-4 yr midpoints (categories 6-7) are common across O*NET. EDA §Implications: 'Tier distribution sanity (P1, new): none of the 4 tiers should be zero.' Threshold: distinct tier count = 4.
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §Implications for DQ Rules + §6. Known-Value Spot Checks

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: spot check 11-1011 tier = 'senior'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code = '11-1011' AND experience_tier != 'senior'
rules.description: BLS SOC 11-1011 (Chief Executives) must resolve to experience_tier = 'senior'. This is a known-value spot check: real-data RW distribution is 68.24% at category 11 (Over 10 years, midpoint 12 years), weighted median = 11, tier = senior.
rules.rationale: EDA §6: '11-1011.00 Chief Executives: Weighted median = 11 (cumulative at cat 11 = 100.0). Midpoint = 12 yr. Tier = senior. Spec assumption confirmed.' Single-row deterministic check. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: spot check 15-1252 tier = 'mid'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code = '15-1252' AND experience_tier != 'mid'
rules.description: BLS SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers) must resolve to experience_tier = 'mid'. Real-data weighted median = category 9 (Over 6 years up to 8, midpoint 7 years) -> mid tier (4-8 yr threshold).
rules.rationale: EDA §6: '15-1252.00 Software Developers: Weighted median = category 9 (midpoint 7 yr). Tier = mid (4-8 yr threshold). Confirmed.' Single-row deterministic check. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: onet_experience_profiles: spot check 41-2031 tier = 'entry'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.onet_experience_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code = '41-2031' AND experience_tier != 'entry'
rules.description: BLS SOC 41-2031 (Retail Salespersons) must resolve to experience_tier = 'entry'. NOTE: real-data RW distribution is bimodal (cat 1 = 39.75%, cat 5 = 32.02%), weighted median = category 5, midpoint 0.75 yr -- which is still < 1 yr, so tier = 'entry'. DO NOT write a 'median_category <= 3' rule for this SOC -- it would fail.
rules.rationale: EDA §6: '41-2031.00 Retail Salespersons: Bimodal! Cumulative crosses 50% at category 5, not 1-3. Weighted median = category 5. Midpoint = 0.75 yr. Tier = entry (0-1 yr threshold) -- spec's tier expectation passes.' EDA explicit warning: 'Do NOT add a median_category <= 3 rule for 41-2031 -- it would fail.' Single-row deterministic tier check. Threshold: 0 violations.
rules.source_spec_ref: docs/specs/onet-experience-requirements.md §Zone 2 DQ Rules (spot checks) + §Zone 2 note on bimodal distribution
rules.source_eda_ref: governance/eda/raw-onet-experience-eda.md §6. Known-Value Spot Checks (41-2031) + §Implications for Spot Checks
